explanations: Feminine nouns ending in -u are declined similarly to the noun 'dhenu' (cow). The declension shows distinct singular and plural forms across all cases.

In the singular, the nominative and vocative are 'dhenu'; the accusative adds -ṃ to give 'dhenuṃ'; the instrumental, ablative, dative, genitive, and locative all take the suffix -yā, giving 'dhenuyā', with the locative also allowing the form 'dhenuyaṃ'.

In the plural, the nominative, vocative, and accusative take either -ū or -yo, giving 'dhenū' or 'dhenuyo'; the instrumental and ablative take -hi (or optionally -bhi), giving 'dhenūhi' or 'dhenūbhi'; the dative and genitive take -naṃ, giving 'dhenūnaṃ'; and the locative takes -su, giving 'dhenūsu'.

Other feminine nouns declined in the same pattern include: yāgu (gruel), kāsu (pit), vijju (lightning), rajju (rope), daddu (eczema), kaṇeru (cow-elephant), dhātu (element, relic), sassu (mother-in-law), and vadhu (daughter-in-law).

tables.title: Declension of dhenu (cow) - Feminine noun ending in -u

pali_to_english: Vadhū sassuyā dhenuṃ rajjuyā bandhitvā khettaṃ nesi.
Ammā yāguṃ pacitvā dārakānaṃ datvā mañce nisīdi.
Yuvatiyā hattesu ca aṇgulīsu ca daddu atthi.
Mayaṃ aṭaviyaṃ carantiyo kaṇeruyo apassimha.
Itthī yuvatiyā bhattaṃ pacāpetvā dārikānaṃ thokaṃ thokaṃ vibhaji.
Tumhe vijjuyā ālokena guhāyam sayantam sīhaṃ passittha.
Yuvatiyā hatthesu kumārehi dinnā mālāyo santi.
Vadhū khette kāsūsu patitāni phalāni saṃhari.
Brāhmaṇo Buddhassa dhātuyo vibhajitvā bhūpālānaṃ adadi / adāsi.
Vadhū sassuyā pāde vandi.
Yuvatiyā geham sammajjitabbaṃ hoti.
Devatāyo sakalaṃ (entire) vihāraṃ obhāsentiyo Buddhaṃ upasaṅkamiṃsu.
Aṭavīsu vasantiyo kaṇeruyo sākhāyo bhañjitvā khādanti.
Ahaṃ rukkhassa chāyāyaṃ nisinnānaṃ dhenūnaṃ ca goṇānaṃ ca tiṇāni adadiṃ / adāsiṃ.
Itthī magge gacchantiṃ ammaṃ passitvā rathamhā oruyha taṃ vanditvā rathasmiṃ āropetvā gehaṃ nesi.
Vadhū gehassa dvāram thaketvā nahāyituṃ nadiṃ upasaṅkamitvā yuvatīhi saddhiṃ sallapantī nadiyā tīre aṭṭhāsi.
Bhūpālo manusse vihiṃsante core nāsetvā dipaṃ pālesi.
Ammā asappurise bhajamāne putte samaṇehi ovādāpesi.
Sappurisena kiṇitvā āhaṭehi bhaṇḍehi chaḍḍetabbaṃ natthi.
Mā tumhe gāme vasante kassake vihiṃsatha.
english_to_pali: The mother took the gold kept in the box and gave it to the daughter.
The daughter-in-law honoured the gods with garlands and fruits.
If you dig holes, I will plant trees.
You (pl.) go to the field and bring the corn home.
Cow-elephants wandered in the forest eating plantain trees.
I looked at the girls crossing the river by boat.
Young women pulled the branches fallen in the pit.
The rays of the sun illuminate the world.
Singing songs the sisters went to the tank to bathe.
The woman tied the cow with a rope and brought it to the field.
The daughter-in-law went to Anurādhapura with the mother-in-law to honour the relics of the Buddha.
May virtue and wisdom illuminate the minds of men in the world.
